title: Unorthodox plays and scary team coordination: Team WE will likely qualify for MSI
summary: Unorthodox plays and scary team coordination: Team WE will likely qualify for MSI  Esports Insider
content:
Davide Xu is a freelance writer at Esports Insider focused on League of Legends esports. He covers everything inside and outside the Rift—especially when it comes to European and Asian competitive scenes. With a finance background and a multicultural lens, he loves talking about business as much as macro.

Olivia Richman is a seasoned esports journalist who has worked with Inven Global, Esports Illustrated, Esports.gg, and more. As an editor and writer at Esports Insider, she loves telling unique esports stories, especially within the FGC. When not working and gaming, Olivia loves collecting Kirby plush, eating sushi, and driving her cars at the track.

If there is any team you should be rooting for right now inLeague of Legendsesports, it should beTeam WE. This mid-pack team pulled off not one, but two of the greatest upsets of the season and are now one series away from qualifying for theMid-Season Invitational.

While everyone loves an underdog story, Team WE’s success feels different. Once considered one of the faces of Chinese esports, the team is far from its glorious days. Could this be the start of a new chapter? And are they one of the most dangerous teams to face in the world, right now?

At the start of the season, Team WE had formed a roster that didn’t really stand out from the pack. It kept its top side trio ofDai “Cube” Yi, Wang “Monki” Meng-Qi, andKim “Karis” Hong-joand brought in a new bot lane with Korean ADCMoon “About” Hyeong-seokandChen “yaoyao” Si-Yuan, following the departure of Kim “Taeyoon” Tae-yoon and Zhao “Vampire” Zhe-can.

The first split of the 2026 season went by without many surprises, with Team WE constantly in the mid-pack. Their top-eight finish, however, allowed them to be placed with the big boys in Group Ascend for the second split.

title: LoL MSI 2026: Which Teams Are Most Likely To Qualify?
summary: LoL MSI 2026: Which Teams Are Most Likely To Qualify?  Hotspawn
content:
The League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational (LoL MSI) 2026 is fast approaching, and fans around the world are preparing for the second international tournament of the season.

First Stand gave us an early look at which are the best teams across the world, with BiliBili Gaming defeating G2 Esports in the final. But with more teams involved and tighter races across all regions, MSI is set to be another explosive international tournament. In this article, we’ll break down which teams are most likely to qualify from each regional league, setting up the expectations of who should be at the event.

The League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational is the second international tournament on the LoL Esports calendar in 2026. This year, the event will take place in Daejeon, South Korea. The format will be similar to last year’s edition, although some changes have been brought in for the play-in stage. The tournament will begin with a four-team double-elimination bracket, featuring the second seeds from the LCK, LEC, LCS, and LCP. Only one team will then join the top eight in the bracket stage.

After winning First Stand, the LPL was given direct qualification to the bracket stage for both seeds, giving them a small advantage and reward for the strong international performance shown earlier in the year. The winner of MSI will not only win the title but also earn a direct Worlds qualification. Moreover, the best two regional leagues will also receive an additional slot for the season-end championship.

Here’s a region-by-region breakdown of the teams that have a higher chance of making it.

Despite seeing some upsets here and there, the powerhouses in the LCK have once again locked out the top three. This time, however, it’s HLE who ended up being the #1 seed, followed byT1, and then Gen.G. The first two will face off for a direct MSI qualification match, with the loser getting a second chance in the lower bracket.

title: Top esports team of May 2026: 11-game unbeaten run spurs T1
summary: Top esports team of May 2026: 11-game unbeaten run spurs T1  Esports Charts
content:
With qualification for these crucial international events ongoing, as many as six esports teams surpassed the 10 million Hours Watched (HW) mark in May 2026, according to Esports Charts. However, only one side,T1, attracted enough audience engagement to go past 15 million HW.

Having taken a back seat to other teams who were busier in terms of competitive engagements earlier in the year, T1 reclaimed its position as the most-watched esports team in April 2026. The reigningLeague of Legendsworld champion repeated that feat in May 2026, accumulating 16.93 million HW.

TheLoL Champions Korea (LCK)powerhouse played the joint-third most games among the top ten on the leaderboard, participating in 12 matches across two competitions. Interestingly, the second-placed side,Counter-Strike 2heavyweightTeam Falcons, also played the same number of series across an equal number of events.Luckily, the sheer interestsurroundingT1 helped itcomfortablyfinish at the top.

Those interested in tracking the performances of sides across esports titles can check out ourTeams Dashboard.This subscriptiongivesusers access toteams-focusedviewership analyticscoveringall matches and broadcasts, includingco-streamers.

T1 began May 2026 with anLCK 2026 Seasonclash againstHanwha Life Esports, who clinched the series 2:1. Even though T1 lost, it remains the second-most popular game so far at this Korean regional league, having reached 1.21 Peak Viewers (PV).

The defending three-time LoL world champion then went on an 11-game unbeaten run across May 2026. It started with a victory againstBRIONin theEsports World Cup 2026 - Korea Qualifier, and the side continued to pick up steam as it clinched wins over other top names likeGen.GandKT Rolster.

title: LoL Esports
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'LoL Esports'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'LoL Esports  LoL Esports' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: LCK 2026 MSI Slot & Esports World Cup Qualification
summary: LCK 2026 MSI Slot & Esports World Cup Qualification  Esports Charts
content:
After drawing more than 1.2 million Peak Viewers during the LCK 2026 Season group stage, T1 and Hanwha Life Esports are set to clash again at theLCK 2026 Road to MSI. With spots at both the Mid-Season Invitational and the Esports World Cup on the line, viewership for this powerhouse matchup could climb even higher.

The LCK 2026 Road to MSI serves as the playoff stage for South Korea’s regional League of Legends spring split, giving teams a direct route to mid-season international competition. The event features the region’s top six teams, with the top two seeds starting in the upper bracket and immediately entering a qualifying match for MSI. The remaining four teams face a much tougher path, beginning in the lower bracket, where every series is an elimination match.

The LCK 2026 Road to MSI is scheduled to run from June 6 to 14, with matches hosted in Seoul and Wonju. To match the high stakes, every series throughout the tournament will feature a Best-of-5 format.

KT Rolstervs Round 1 winner @ 10:00 CET / June 7, 2026.

T1vsHanwha Life Esports@ 10:00 CET / June 12, 2026.The winner will be qualified for MSI 2026 and Esports World Cup 2026.

Gen.Gvs Round 2 winner @ 10:00 CET / June 13, 2026.
